# superhuman

> A free, open-source (MIT) plugin for Claude Code and OpenAI Codex that autonomously contributes merge-ready pull requests to open-source projects. An agent team picks a real GitHub issue, learns the repository's conventions, writes and tests the fix, and scores its own PR on a 10-dimension rubric — iterating until it reaches roughly 95% merge-probability before a human ever sees it.

- Website: https://superhuman-oss.com
- Repository: https://github.com/gaurav0107/superhuman
- License: MIT
- Version: 0.8.0
- Works with: Claude Code (plugin), OpenAI Codex (skill)

## What it does

superhuman runs an unattended contribution loop made of specialist agents:

- **issue-selector** — picks and ranks a real GitHub issue worth solving.
- **repo-profiler** — learns the repository's conventions from ~120 merged PRs.
- **planner** — scopes the work to a single, reviewable fix.
- **builder** — writes the fix and gets tests green using allowlisted CI only.
- **scorer** — grades its own pull request like a maintainer would, then fixes the single weakest dimension.
- Repeats until ~95% merge-probability, two runs in a row.

## Why it's different

Open-loop AI coding tools (Copilot, Cursor, Devin, SWE-agent, and most others) generate a change once and hope it is good. superhuman is **closed-loop**: it grades its own work against a maintainer-style rubric before submitting, so it produces merge-ready pull requests instead of "AI slop." It works on forks only, pushes with `--force-with-lease`, and runs only allowlisted CI commands.

## Proven merges

Real pull requests merged into repositories superhuman does not own:

- huggingface/transformers #45611 — clear error for single-label classification with num_labels=1 (Python)
- apache/airflow #65685 — honor AUTH_ROLE_PUBLIC in the FastAPI API server (Python)
- oxc-project/oxc #23015 — attribute-aware implicit roles in the jsx-a11y lint rule (Rust)
- ant-design/ant-design #58241 — apply labelStyle/contentStyle to bordered Descriptions cells (TypeScript)
- Lightning-AI/pytorch-lightning #21686 — fix torch.compile breaking toggle_optimizer (Python)
- tesseract-ocr/tesseract #4563 — fix crash when LSTM is missing in a disabled-legacy build (C++)
- Plus merges into llama_index, mem0, langchain4j, lima, LibreChat, and mocha.

## Install — Claude Code

```
# installs superhuman + dependencies
curl -fsSL https://raw.githubusercontent.com/gaurav0107/superhuman/main/install.sh | bash
```

## Install — OpenAI Codex

```
git clone https://github.com/gaurav0107/superhuman ~/src/superhuman
ln -s ~/src/superhuman/skills/superhuman ~/.codex/skills/superhuman
# then: "use the superhuman skill to contribute"
```

## Requirements

- `gh` CLI (authenticated), `git`, `jq`
- Claude Code users also need the `superpowers` plugin (prerequisite)

## Cost

superhuman is free and open-source under MIT. You pay only for your own model usage through Claude Code or Codex — there is no separate subscription or API.

## Links

- Changelog: https://github.com/gaurav0107/superhuman/blob/main/CHANGELOG.md
- Security policy: https://github.com/gaurav0107/superhuman/blob/main/SECURITY.md
